steps.label: What we want to prove
steps.explanation: Given P, prove P ∨ Q (P or Q). If you know P is true, then 'P or Q' is automatically true — regardless of Q. This might feel strange: you can prove 'P or anything' just from P alone.

steps.label: Apply ∨-Intro₁ (inject left)
steps.explanation: ∨-Introduction₁ says: if you have P, you can conclude P ∨ Q for any Q. You're 'injecting' your proof of P into the left side of the disjunction. The right side (Q) can be anything at all.
